title: Vriesea breviscapa
taxonomy: Vriesea breviscapa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. This family includes a wide variety of plants, many of which are epiphytes, meaning they grow on other plants without deriving nutrients from them. Vriesea breviscapa is a type of tillandsia, a genus of epiphytic plants within the Bromeliaceae family. The plant is native to South America and is known for its striking appearance and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. It is often cultivated and prized for its unique and attractive foliage.

intro: Vriesea breviscapa, also known as the Short-scapa Vriesea, is a species of bromeliad, a type of epiphytic plant native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Vriesea breviscapa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Vriesea breviscapa is often used in traditional herbal medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote wound healing and support immune function.
